{{Quote|Crates of gold and guns and garnets, green hides from Saheelindeel, the mending mud of Draflago, and quids of juicy chak-root, packing the pincher-horn of a W'iiri. The son takes after the father, a pirate to the core.|Xer VIII, according to ''The Pirate Prince''|<ref name="Xim">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/ximweek03/index.html|text=Xim Week: ''The Despotica'' (Part II: The Pirate Prince)|int=Xim Week: The Despotica}}</ref>}}
The '''W'iiri''' were a six-[[Limb/Legends|legged]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with horn plating and pincers. A number of W'iiri worked for [[J'uoch]], the leader of a criminal [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operation, on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dellalt/Legends|Dellalt]]. They were present when [[J'uoch's mining camp]] came under attack from reactivated [[war-robot]]s that had been part of [[Xim/Legends|Xim's]] [[Guardian Corps]]; three W'iiri [[Death/Legends|died]] while defending the camp against the attack.

==Biology and appearance==
The W'iiri were a horn-plated [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with dangerous pincer-horns. They had six [[Limb/Legends|legs]], on which they appeared to scuttle.<ref name="HLL14" />

==History==
{{Quote|We've got no time to debate this, J'uoch; you and your whole camp are about to come under attack!|Han Solo|<ref name="HLL14" />}}
The W'iiri were mentioned in ''[[The Pirate Prince]]'', a dramatization of the conquests of [[Xim/Legends|Xim]] the Despot written by the [[Lisst'n]] dramatist [[Lyechusas]], circa [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]]. It included a W'iiri pincher-horn among the spoils of the Despot's conquests.<ref name="Xim" />

[[File:GuardianCorps-FF60.png|thumb|right|upright|200px|Three W'iiri were killed by one Guardian Corps war robot.]]
During the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], a number of W'iiri worked for [[J'uoch]], the leader of a criminal [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operation, on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dellalt/Legends|Dellalt]]. In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] tried to sneak up on [[J'uoch's mining camp]], but he inadvertently triggered a [[sensor net]]. One of the W'iiri, being a trusted employee, was issued a [[Grenade launcher/Legends|grenade launcher]] and joined a response [[Squad/Legends|squad]] to attack Solo. The rest of the response squad was composed of two [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and a [[Drall (planet)|Drall]]; all were forced to seek cover when Solo began firing on them with a [[Kell Mark II heavy assault rifle]]. The W'iiri and its companions later tried to work their way up the hill on which Solo was located under cover fire from their companions. Nevertheless, they were again forced back by the Human.<ref name="HLL14" />

Shortly after, when the mining camp was attacked by remnants of [[Xim/Legends|Xim's]] [[Guardian Corps]] activated by [[BLX-5|Bollux]], a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] companion of Solo, another three W'iiri worked together to attack one of the droids. By tearing at the droid with their pincers, they were able to do enough to destroy it, but not before it smashed each of them, tossing them aside to [[Death/Legends|die]].<ref name="HLL14" /> Shortly after their sacrifice, the remainder of the droids were defeated; however, not before they overran the camp.<ref name="EGD">''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The W'iiri first appeared ''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]'', a [[1980]] novel written by [[Brian Daley]].<ref name="HLL14" />
